note: Canonical model registry: what each model CLAIMS (specs/capabilities) + how it should be used. Source of truth for the desktop Models definition page. The 3-day model-discovery cron appends/updates entries; new claims land in the Inbox for review.

models.use: candidate worker brain — free, good for swarm bulk + long-context reasoning lanes
default worker brain (all lanes currently)
high-value orchestration/design only — scarce
coding worker brain candidate
local fallback / privacy / offline
local worker brain candidate (private/offline, Mac-mini host) — pull via `ollama pull gemma4`
models.note: surfaced by Fritz; was in the original PDF report I could not extract

models.use: NOT a model brain — Google's agentic coding platform. Evaluate as a tool/competitor + possible integration target, not a worker lane
multimodal candidate — vision/audio for the vision-QA gate + mobile/glasses layer; confirm exact id/availability

claims.cost: FREE via Hermes Portal (inside Hermes Agent)

models.use: free worker brain candidate via the Hermes Portal provider — verify provider config + exact model id, then wire to a lane
models.note: surfaced by Fritz 2026-05-31 | PARKED 2026-05-31: Nous Portal is subscription-gated + device-code login returned 400; not pursuing now.

models.use: TOP pick for the CODING lane — strongest free coding model, 1M ctx; rate-limited so use for non-bursty code-gen / fallback to gpt-5.3-codex
TOP pick for the RESEARCH / REASONING lane — native reasoning, 1M ctx, free; pairs with Qwen3-Coder for build+reason swarm
AGENTIC / TOOL-USE lane candidate — strongest open agentic model; vision-capable for the vision-QA gate
general-purpose / coding fallback lane — also runnable on Groq/Cerebras free tiers for speed
VISION lane candidate — vision+tools, also pullable locally via Ollama (gemma4) for the Mac-mini private lane
MOBILE-EDGE / cheap-bulk lane candidate — small fast model for classification/triage; also free via NVIDIA NIM (build.nvidia.com)
MOBILE-EDGE lane candidate — 1.2B reasoning model small enough for on-device/glasses layer

claims.cost: FREE tier, no credit card (30 req/min, 6K tokens/min, 14.4K req/day)
strengths: open-weight catalog: gpt-oss, llama, qwen, gemma

models.use: SPEED provider — host free open-weight models for latency-sensitive lanes; pair with OpenRouter for failover

claims.cost: FREE tier 1M tokens/day, no credit card (30 req/min, 60-100K tokens/min, 8192 ctx cap on free)

models.use: HIGH-VOLUME FREE provider — best daily token budget for bulk swarm work; note 8K context cap on free tier limits long-context lanes

claims.cost: FREE tier (Flash ~15 RPM / 1M TPM / 1500 RPD; Pro ~2 RPM / 32K TPM)

models.use: LONG-CONTEXT + VISION free lane — Gemini Flash free tier complements the existing paid gemini-2.5-flash default; OAuth, no card. Free limits cut 50-80% in Dec 2025
free hosting for Nemotron lanes (reasoning/long-context) without burning OpenRouter daily quota

claims.cost: Free tier $0.10/month credit, no markup on provider rates
strengths: trending: GLM-4.7, DeepSeek-V4, Kimi K2.6, Qwen3, SmolLM3-3B

models.use: discovery/eval surface — cheap way to test trending open models before wiring a lane; tiny monthly credit so not for production volume

_provider_free_caps.note: Free-tier caps for the free-limit counter to track. Verify/update via the 3-day discovery cron.

openrouter:free.scope: shared across ALL :free models on the key

nous-portal.note: subscription, not free; free-for-subscribers rotating list

free_limit_counter.desc: Track per-model/provider usage (rpm/rpd/tpd) vs caps above; surface near-limit + exhausted; feed credential-pool rotation + low-funds notification.

build_lane_policy.note: Mid-tier build brains (Kimi/DeepSeek on OpenRouter) before falling to capped Claude. Fritz OK spending OpenRouter credits on builds.
build_lane_policy.swarm_scale_goal: 300 agents — spread across OpenRouter+Cerebras+Groq+NIM+Ollama to dodge per-provider rate caps (no hard ceiling exists).
